eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus crowns Zach Werenski the clear Norris Trophy frontrunner at nearly 59% implied probability, driven by his league-leading 43 points (9 goals, 34 assists) among defensemen through 31 games, fueling Columbus's surprising playoff push. If the listed player is not announced as a finalist for the 2025–26 Vezina Trophy, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the NHL. However, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Tampa Bay Lightning's Andrei Vasilevskiy at 63% implied probability for the Vezina Trophy, propelled by his league-leading 2.28 goals-against average and .921 save percentage across 44 games since returning from a midseason injury, anchoring the Lightning's surge into playoff contention. New York Islanders' Ilya Sorokin holds 30% with consistent elite play (2.41 GAA, .910 SV% in 54 outings), bolstering a middling team amid trade deadline reinforcements. Boston's Jeremy Swayman at 7% benefits from the Bruins' record-tying 61 wins and his .897 SV% in tandem with Linus Ullmark, though shared duties temper his case. Recent hot streaks—Vasilevskiy's 1.79 GAA over his last 10 starts—have widened the gap, while voters weigh full-season volume against late momentum as the regular season nears its April 18 close.
